mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales transmit into higher expected corporate revenues, pushing equity valuations upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive following month-over-month RSAFS increases (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6.5% signals excess labor market slack that reduces household income growth and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC is positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% combined with an unemployment rate above 6.0% reflects aggressive monetary accommodation in response to economic weakness, which compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of joint-signal periods over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during early-cycle expansions indicates improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive during early expansion windows (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.0% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs and equity discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0%, or the Rank IC turns positive over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based aggregate demand strength that lifts corporate top-line growth expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between the joint demand score and 20-day returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which the market prices into near-term earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive following month-over-month PAYEMS increases (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1000 thousand units indicate depressed residential investment and construction activity, foreshadowing weaker aggregate demand and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1000, falsifying the negative prediction.
